text: Git branching is an essential part of version control that helps teams work on different tasks simultaneously. A common workflow involves creating feature branches, which are separate copies of the main code where developers can add new functions or fix bugs without affecting the stable version. Once the work on the feature branch is complete, the team can merge these changes back into the main branch. Before merging, many teams use pull requests to review the code, discuss improvements, and ensure quality. This workflow keeps the project organized, prevents conflicts, and allows multiple developers to collaborate efficiently without disrupting each other’s progress.
